schema_ownership.review_authority: Shared Schema Package maintainers using SDS #7 plus owning service SDS refs
schema_ownership.consumer_notes: Canonical JSON Schema and Rust projection metadata for CLI, local stack, and integration harness contract slices; later released external consumers require compatibility and migration gates.

schema_ownership.review_authority: Shared Schema Package maintainers using SDS #7 Phase 2
schema_ownership.consumer_notes: Phase 2 canonical source layout and common primitive metadata; JSON Schema remains the authority and Rust projection stays non-authoritative.

guardrails: Canonical schema source lives under approved packages/schemas roots and generated outputs stay outside source directories.
Public object references must use typed ref primitives and must not fall back to unstructured strings.
Lifecycle primitives must include timestamp, logical sequence, schema version, command id, trace id, idempotency key, request hash, payload hash, compatibility window, and deprecation metadata.
Privacy classes must distinguish public, tenant-private, regulated, encrypted/private, user-content, system-service-only, and redacted diagnostic data.
Reason-code entries must be documented, Rust-first enum sources with retryability, correction fields, dependency names, policy refs, audit refs, and unsupported-version metadata.

schema_ownership.review_authority: Shared Schema Package maintainers using SDS #7 Phase 3 with owning Phase 1 service review
schema_ownership.consumer_notes: Phase 3 contract-module metadata for identity/tenant, command/API error, event/audit, manifest/registry, and queue/lease/credential/key shapes. JSON Schema remains the authority and Rust projection stays non-authoritative.

guardrails: Identity and tenant module records require tenant, actor, organization, role, delegation, quota, suspension, catalog visibility, and audit-context refs while rejecting raw secrets or credential values.

Command envelopes require tenant id, actor id, command type, trace id, idempotency key, timestamp, schema version, payload type, payload hash, and signature metadata; API errors require reason code, trace id, retryability, and correction shape.
Event and audit module records are append-only, privacy-classified, evidence-linked, and reject raw private payload leakage.

Manifest and registry module records require schema versions, typed secret refs, policy refs, resource/data refs, retry policy, and reject untyped capability blobs or private package internals as public schema authority.

Queue, lease, credential, and key metadata stays ref-only and must preserve trace, tenant, actor, command, retry, timeout, cancellation, completion, heartbeat, signer, rotation, and revocation metadata without private key material.

schema_ownership.review_authority: Shared Schema Package maintainers using SDS #7 Phase 4 with Rust-first generation review
schema_ownership.consumer_notes: Phase 4 generation toolchain metadata for Rust-first generated/projection outputs, generated docs, blocked TypeScript/web projections, and internal-only Protobuf projections. JSON Schema remains the authority and all generated outputs stay non-authoritative.

guardrails: Generation commands must be deterministic dry-run checks over canonical JSON Schema and manifest inputs.
Rust generated/projection outputs are validated first and must include validator entrypoints, schema-version constants, reason-code metadata, and redaction metadata.
Generated docs must carry source-to-doc trace metadata and cannot become the contract authority.
TypeScript/web projections remain blocked until Rust projections, golden fixtures, docs trace, and compatibility checks are stable.
Protobuf projections are internal-only and must reject Protobuf-only public object definitions.

schema_ownership.owning_service_family: SDS #7 Shared Schema Package and SDS #4 Local Development Stack
schema_ownership.review_authority: Shared Schema Package maintainers with Local Development Stack service owner review
schema_ownership.consumer_notes: Phase-gated local/test schema module metadata; canonical JSON Schema remains source of truth and Rust projection stays non-authoritative.

guardrails: Local Development Stack Phase 2 contracts must keep stack profiles, service definitions, ports, env manifests, volumes, reset plans, seed manifests, health snapshots, local secrets, and diagnostics local/test-only.
Ports must bind to loopback hosts in the reserved 18080-18085 range and reject collisions before startup.
Future services must remain gated by owning-service phase states and must not introduce production endpoints, external cloud resources, or non-Overrid product boundaries.
Reset and seed contracts must require deterministic local/test markers and must not contain raw secrets or non-test fixture material.
Health and diagnostic records must stay secret-free and use stable local stack health states and reason codes.

schema_ownership.owning_service_family: SDS #7 Shared Schema Package and SDS #3 Integration Test Harness
schema_ownership.review_authority: Shared Schema Package maintainers with Integration Test Harness service owner review
schema_ownership.consumer_notes: Phase-gated integration harness schema module metadata; fixture and artifact contracts remain canonical JSON Schema with Rust projection checks only.

schema_ownership.owning_service_family: SDS #7 Shared Schema Package and SDS #3 Integration Test Harness
schema_ownership.review_authority: Shared Schema Package maintainers with Integration Test Harness service owner review
schema_ownership.consumer_notes: Phase-gated integration harness Phase 6 module metadata; step-runner artifacts stay redacted and schema-owned.

guardrails: Scenario step runners must use CLI, SDK, API, local helper, and assertion paths without direct private storage reads or direct service URLs.
Blocked step outcomes must stay blocked and failed step outcomes must retain failure evidence artifacts.
Local helper actions are limited to stack lifecycle, seed verification, artifact lookup, event export, and diagnostic checks.

schema_ownership.owning_service_family: SDS #7 Shared Schema Package and SDS #3 Integration Test Harness
schema_ownership.review_authority: Shared Schema Package maintainers with Integration Test Harness service owner review
schema_ownership.consumer_notes: Phase-gated integration harness Phase 7 module metadata; exact protocol, DAG, policy, and accounting traces stay schema-owned and secret-free.

guardrails: Golden trace assertions must check exact Phase 0/1 protocol event order, partially ordered DAG edges, forbidden transitions, schema versions, and stable reason-code vocabulary.
Execution-loop traces must cover queue, scheduler, lease, runner, result, retry, cancellation, timeout, dead-letter, usage, and audit nodes through local fixture refs only.
Policy, verification, dispute, usage, ledger, and receipt traces must remain secret-free and expose authorized refs without direct service, storage, ledger, payment, or pricing assumptions.

schema_ownership.owning_service_family: SDS #7 Shared Schema Package and SDS #3 Integration Test Harness
schema_ownership.review_authority: Shared Schema Package maintainers with Integration Test Harness service owner review
schema_ownership.consumer_notes: Phase-gated integration harness Phase 8 module metadata; artifact, redaction, reproduction, and flake evidence stay schema-owned.

guardrails: Artifact collection must expose redacted logs, Overwatch exports, CLI outputs, API payload envelopes, service health snapshots, fixture versions, schema versions, assertion diffs, scenario ids, trace roots, and stable reason codes.

Redaction scanner evidence must fail closed for raw secrets, tokens, private keys, signatures, private payloads, decrypted RAG content, fixture key material, private file contents, and unsafe local paths.
Reproduction commands must include profile, fixture manifest, scenario id, trace root when applicable, artifact output path, and preserve failed scenario reason-code classification on rerun.
Flake metadata must capture repeated run count, timing variance, nondeterministic assertion markers, unstable event ordering, and tolerance-window use without marking nondeterminism as success.
Retention classes must distinguish compact successful smoke, failure evidence, release candidates, and phase-gate evidence without adding non-Rust runtime authority.

schema_ownership.owning_service_family: SDS #7 Shared Schema Package and SDS #3 Integration Test Harness
schema_ownership.review_authority: Shared Schema Package maintainers with Integration Test Harness service owner review
schema_ownership.consumer_notes: Phase-gated integration harness Phase 9 module metadata; CI and product integration evidence remains contract-checked and phase-gated.

guardrails: Phase and tag selection must inherit earlier master phase gates and only select mandatory gate classes for the requested phase.
CI smoke selection must prefer explicit ci_smoke scenarios and keep success, blocked, and failed outcomes machine-readable through stable exit classes.
Service contract coverage must report service ids, phases, scenario ids, schema modules, event types, reason-code families, and missing required contracts.
Product integration gates for Docdex, Mcoda, Codali, CLI, SDK, and admin UI remain planned until owning contracts exist and must route through public Overrid contracts.

Later deployment, federation, public-pool, native-app, governance, incident, migration, reporting, and scale gates must remain planned or missing-required-contract evidence until their owning phase contracts exist.

schema_ownership.owning_service_family: SDS #7 Shared Schema Package and SDS #3 Integration Test Harness
schema_ownership.review_authority: Shared Schema Package maintainers with Integration Test Harness service owner review
schema_ownership.consumer_notes: Phase-gated integration harness Phase 10 module metadata; handoff and downstream readiness evidence stays schema-owned and non-authoritative for runtime services.

guardrails: Harness self-validation must cover manifest loading, fixture generation, local stack lifecycle, scenario execution, assertion records, golden traces, artifact export, redaction, and phase selection.
Tech-stack alignment must keep harness ownership Rust-first, fixture surfaces JSON/JSON Schema based, and local substitutes Overrid-shaped.
Security and redaction checks must prove fixture credentials stay test_only, artifact bundles stay secret-free, non-local profiles are blocked, and direct private storage reads are denied.
Docs and progress evidence must keep the SDS, build plan, phase plan, phase progress, wrapper validation, and Docdex memory aligned.

Downstream handoff requires service builders to add scenario manifests, fixture refs, golden traces or assertion refs, redacted artifact expectations, and service contract coverage before marking integration-ready.
JSON Schema is the docs-facing and fixture-facing source of truth.
The Rust projection is consumed by the CLI and SDK but must not become contract authority.
CLI runtime remains Rust-first and targets Overgate through SDK contracts.
Fixtures must preserve final output-envelope and Overgate-only validation shapes.
Profiles and credential references must be secret-free and environment-scoped before request construction.
Signer handoff returns signature refs only and never exposes raw key material.
Command lifecycle, exit classes, diagnostics, and capability snapshots must be rendered from the canonical output envelope.
Phase 1 bootstrap commands must construct signed SDK/Overgate envelopes and must not call Overtenant, Overpass, Overkey, Overregistry, Overqueue, or Overwatch directly.
Canonical idempotency fingerprints must include environment, endpoint identity, tenant, actor, command, target, canonical payload hash, expected current state, reason, and schema version.
Retry and timeout policy must stay bounded, classify retryable transport/platform failures, and keep denials non-retryable.
Error decode records must expose stable reason codes, retry classes, exit classes, and remediation hints without raw internal errors.
Local idempotency cache records must be scoped to profile/environment, owner-only, inspectable/resettable, and free of private payloads.
Phase 7 node status records must be profile-scoped, credential-checked, capability-ref backed, and free of direct node access.
Phase 7 workload execution timelines must use Overgate/Overqueue/Oversched/Overlease/Overrun/Overwatch refs and expose trace-linked diagnostics only.
Phase 7 log/result retrieval must expose authorized refs, bounded streaming or polling plans, and redacted outputs without private storage paths.
Phase 8 policy dry-run commands must remain dry-run/read-only and route through SDK/Overgate contracts without direct policy-service access.
Phase 8 package validation commands must expose schema/signature/hash/dependency/permission/SBOM/provenance/policy compatibility refs without direct package-store access.
Phase 8 usage, receipt, ledger, and dispute read commands must expose authorized read-model refs without direct meter, ledger, dispute, or payment mutation access.
Phase 8 accounting outputs must not encode pricing, revenue, customer-count, market-volume, blockchain, or NFT assumptions.
Phase 9 product integration workflows for Docdex, Mcoda, and Codali must route through CLI/SDK/Overgate recipes and authorized refs, not direct internal APIs.

Phase 9 Mcoda product workflows must keep model, provider, resource, tool-boundary, budget, and usage metadata dynamic without hardcoded model, node, paid-service, raw HTTP, or private-storage assumptions.
Phase 9 Codali product workflows must expose repository context, logs, artifacts, structured results, retry/repair boundaries, and per-agent-phase usage through authorized refs only.
Phase 9 CI automation profiles must use explicit ci profile kinds and short-lived or mounted credential refs with stable secret-free JSON output and no ambient persistent keychain defaults.
Phase 10 release-readiness output must validate contract snapshots, help text, exit classes, reason-code families, JSON/human output, and error decoding without changing earlier command contracts.

Phase 10 security validation must keep credential storage, signer handoff, diagnostics, logs, results, and handoff output secret-free with no raw keys, tokens, signatures, private payloads, decrypted content, unsafe endpoints, or cross-tenant shortcuts.
Phase 10 phase-availability validation must keep unavailable later services denied with stable not_available_in_phase reason codes and no private shortcuts.
Phase 10 Phase 7/13 handoff evidence must not authorize high-risk governance, compliance, incident, break-glass, migration, or backbone operations before owning contracts exist.
Integration Harness Phase 2 contracts must keep fixture, scenario, run-record, assertion, golden-trace, and artifact-bundle schemas as canonical JSON Schema sources with Rust projection checks only.
Integration Harness Phase 2 fixture credentials must be visibly test_only and must reject raw key material, non-local profiles, unsafe timeouts, unsupported phases, and raw secret artifact bundles.

Integration Harness Phase 2 artifact bundles must name redacted log, Overwatch export, CLI output, API payload envelope, stack health, and fixture version refs, and reject private keys, tokens, signatures, encrypted RAG content, private payloads, and fixture key material.
